commit.message:
Add dhcpmon v6 counter CLI (show/clear) and update tests (#26467)

show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to show dhcpmon v6 counters from COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB wrapper)
clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to clear dhcpmon v6 counters via SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 protocol (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB)
Merged duplicate v4/v6 dhcpmon functions into shared parameterized versions (prefix parameter for table selection)
Fixed v4 clear to include Malformed/Ignored counter types
Added section separators and comments for code organization
Old dhcp6relay commands still available via standalone commands: show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters
Updated unit tests for renamed functions
Breaking Changes
show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now reads dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via show dhcp6relay_counters counts. dhcpmon v6 ships in the same image, so this command will always have data.

clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters now also zeros "Malformed" and "Ignored" counter types. This is intentional — dhcpmon writes these types to COUNTERS_DB, and not zeroing them during the SIGUSR2 cache-sync causes stale values to persist after clear.

clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now clears dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay clear remains accessible via sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters.

Design Notes
SIGUSR signal scope: There is one dhcpmon process per VLAN handling both v4 and v6. SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 pauses all counter writes (both protocols) which is necessary for consistent clear.
Show/clear type asymmetry: show intentionally excludes Malformed/Ignored from display to keep output clean, but clear must zero all types (including Malformed/Ignored) for the SIGUSR cache-sync protocol to work correctly.
Why I did it
sonic-dhcpmon now supports DHCPv6 counters (PR sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing to
DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. The CLI needs matching show/clear commands
so operators can view and reset these counters, and so the test infrastructure
can validate them.

Work item tracking
Microsoft ADO (number only):
How I did it
sonic-dhcpmon added DHCPv6 counter support (sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing per-interface v6 packet counts to DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. However, the CLI had no commands to display or clear these v6 counters. The existing show/clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters commands were thin wrappers around dhcp6relay STATE_DB data, which is a separate counter
source with less granularity (no per-interface breakdown, no per-direction TX/RX).

This PR repurposes those commands to show/clear dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB data instead, giving operators consistent v4/v6 counter visibility through a single CLI interface. The old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via standalone commands (show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters).

How to verify it
Deploy dhcpmon with v6 support on a DUT with DHCPv6 relay configured.
Run show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should display per-VLAN/per-interface
DHCPv6 packet counts from COUNTERS_DB.
Run sonic-clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should zero all v6 counters.
Run show dhcp6relay_counters counts — should still show dhcp6relay
STATE_DB counters independently.
Existing v4 commands (show/clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters) unchanged in behavior.
Unit tests: cd dockers/docker-dhcp-relay/cli-plugin-tests && pytest

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] perf/refactor: convert ILFieldDefs/ILEventDefs/ILPropertyDefs to concrete classes with lazy name-lookup caches (#502)

🤖 *This PR was created by [Repo
Assist](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/main/docs/repo-assist.md),
an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

This PR converts `ILFieldDefs`, `ILEventDefs`, and `ILPropertyDefs` from
abstract interfaces to concrete classes, mirroring the existing
`ILMethodDefs` pattern. It adds lazy O(1) name-lookup caches throughout
and makes entries truly lazy (computed at most once per type).

### Background

Previously these three types were abstract interfaces:

```fsharp
// Before — computed on every .Entries access; linear O(n) name lookups everywhere
type ILFieldDefs =
    abstract Entries: ILFieldDef[]
```

Every name-based lookup (`GetField`, `GetPropertyImpl`, `GetEvent`,
`GetEnumUnderlyingType`) called `Array.tryFind` — an O(n) linear scan.
Additionally, `seekReadEvents` and `seekReadProperties` recomputed the
entries array on every `.Entries` access (the binary reader range scan
ran again each time).

### Changes

**Concrete classes with lazy dict caches** — mirroring `ILMethodDefs`:

```fsharp
type ILFieldDefs(larr: Lazy<ILFieldDef[]>) =
    let lmap = lazy (
        let d = Dictionary<string, ILFieldDef>()
        for f in larr.Force() do d.[f.Name] <- f
        d)
    member __.Entries = larr.Force()
    member __.TryFindByName nm =
        let scc, v = lmap.Value.TryGetValue(nm) in if scc then Some v else None
```

Similarly for `ILEventDefs` and `ILPropertyDefs`.

**Entries are now truly lazy** — `seekReadEvents`/`seekReadProperties`
previously re-ran the range scan on every `.Entries` call. Now the lazy
constructor caches after first access.

**O(1) lookups in `TypeSymbol` and `TargetTypeDefinition`**:
- `TypeSymbol.GetField/GetPropertyImpl/GetEvent` (generic
instantiations): now use `TryFindByName` instead of `Array.tryFind`
- `TargetTypeDefinition.GetField/GetPropertyImpl/GetEvent`: new lazy
`fieldDefsMap`/`propDefsMap`/`eventDefsMap` dictionaries over the
wrapped reflection objects
- `TargetTypeDefinition.GetEnumUnderlyingType`: uses `TryFindByName` for
the `"value__"` field

**All creation sites updated**: empty constants,
`seekReadFields`/`seekReadEvents`/`seekReadProperties`, and
`ProvidedTypeBuilder`.

### Trade-offs

- Slightly higher memory use per loaded `ILTypeDef` (dictionary
overhead), but only allocated on first name lookup
- No API surface changes — `Entries` still works identically;
`TryFindByName` is a new additive method

### Relation to issue #500

This PR implements the code improvements from the blocked issue #500
(which was blocked by the inclusion of `.github/dependabot.yml`). This
PR contains only the code changes, without the Dependabot config.

## Test Status

✅ **126/126 tests pass** (`dotnet test
tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj -c Release --framework
net8.0`)

Build: `dotnet build src/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.fsproj -c Release` —
succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger broken (new ref each call); add 5 tests for warning + enum round-trip (#501)

🤖 *This PR was created by [Repo
Assist](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/main/docs/repo-assist.md),
an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

This PR bundles a **bug fix** and **new tests** (Task 9: Testing
Improvements).

### Bug fix: `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger` was silently broken

The static `Logger` member was implemented as a computed property:

```fsharp
// Before — creates a fresh ref cell on every access; impossible to set
static member Logger: (string -> unit) option ref = ref None
```

Every read of `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger` returned a *different*
`ref` object, so calling `ProvidedTypeDefinition.Logger := Some f` would
write into a temporary cell that is immediately discarded. The Logger
was effectively inoperative: the "all static parameters optional"
warning (added in 8.3.0 / PR #428) could never fire via the Logger, and
type-creation trace messages were silently dropped.

**Fix**: introduces a `static let loggerRef` field and makes `Logger`
return it:

```fsharp
static let loggerRef: (string -> unit) option ref = ref None
...
static member Logger: (string -> unit) option ref = loggerRef
```

### New tests (5 tests, 131 total)

**`BasicErasedProvisionTests.fs` — 3 new tests for the all-optional
warning:**
- `DefineStaticParameters warns when all static parameters have
defaults` — regression for the warning path (which was previously
unreachable due to the Logger bug)
- `DefineStaticParameters does not warn when at least one parameter has
no default`
- `DefineStaticParameters does not warn when there are no static
parameters`

**`GenerativeEnumsProvisionTests.fs` — 2 new tests for non-Int32 enum
round-trip via target context:**
- `Byte enum underlying type is correct when read via target context
(ReadRelatedAssembly)` — exercises
`TargetTypeDefinition.GetEnumUnderlyingType()` for a `byte`-backed enum
- `Int64 enum underlying type is correct when read via target context
(ReadRelatedAssembly)` — same for `int64`-backed enums

These complement the existing runtime-path enum tests (`Assembly.Load`)
by covering the design-time IL-reader path, which was fixed in PR
#470/#475.

## Trade-offs

- The Logger ref is now module-level shared state. Tests that manipulate
it should save/restore the previous value (all three new tests do this
via `try/finally`).

## Test Status

✅ All 131 tests pass (`dotnet test
tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj -c Release`)

commit.message:
feat(skills): show skill source origin in panel cards

Replace generic 'user' source with granular 'project' | 'global' | 'plugin'.
Each SkillCard now shows a small colour-coded label beside the skill name:
- project  (blue)  — from <projectPath>/.agents/skills/
- global   (purple) — from ~/.claude/skills/
- plugin   (amber)  — from a Claude Code installed plugin

scanSkillsDir() now takes an explicit source parameter; call sites pass
'project' or 'global' accordingly. Pencil icon still shown only for
non-plugin sources (both project and global skills remain editable).

commit.message:
feat: real tool loop in AgentHarness::run() (Phase 1b)

AgentHarness::run() now implements a proper agent loop:
- Calls LLM via provider.chat() (non-streaming, gets tool_calls)
- Checks stop_reason: EndTurn → return text, ToolUse → execute tools
- Executes tools via ToolRegistry, feeds results back to history
- Loops until final text response or max_tool_rounds exhausted
- LoopDetector: detects repeated tool calls with same args (threshold 3)
- Circuit breaker: 5 consecutive tool failures → stop with explanation
- CancellationToken checked at each iteration
- TokenBudget warnings emitted at 70%/85%/95%
- Events: tool_call_start, tool_result_end, text_delta, turn_complete
- Tool specs from ToolRegistry injected into ChatRequest.tools
- Proper tool result → history message with tool_use_id
- All 1,079 tests pass (6 test mocks updated for chat() vs stream())

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime): normalize legacy watcher metadata for backward compatibility (#6676)

* fix(runtime): normalize legacy watcher metadata for backward compatibility

* runtime: normalizeWatchers returns undefined

Change normalizeWatchers to return undefined when given no input instead of an empty object. The function signature now allows undefined in the return type and the early return was updated from {} to undefined. This preserves the absence of watchers and aligns the implementation with the updated typing while keeping the existing mapping logic for provided handlers.

* fix(test): return undefined for empty watchers

normalizeWatchers previously returned an empty object for an empty input. Change it to return undefined (matching the behavior for undefined input) and cache Object.keys(raw) to avoid repeated enumeration. Tests updated to expect undefined for both undefined and empty-object inputs.

* Update src/runtime/set-value.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): use fileURLToPath for cross-platform __dirname in wdio config

`new URL(import.meta.url).pathname` produces a leading slash on Windows
(e.g. `/C:/...`) which breaks path resolution. `fileURLToPath` handles
this correctly on all platforms.

* Return same reference for modern-format watchers

Add a fast path in normalizeWatchers to detect when watcher entries use the modern object format (no legacy string handlers) and return the original input reference instead of allocating a new object. This avoids unnecessary allocations during bootstrap. Tests updated to assert identity for modern-format input; behavior for legacy string handlers remains unchanged (normalization still maps legacy strings to handler objects).

* Move normalizeWatchers into its own module

Extract normalizeWatchers from set-value.ts into a dedicated src/runtime/normalize-watchers.ts and remove the duplicate implementation. Update imports across the runtime and hydrate code (bootstrap-custom-element, bootstrap-lazy, initialize-component, proxy-component, runtime/index, hydrate/platform/proxy-host-element) and test to use the new module. The change centralizes watcher normalization (handling legacy string[] watcher format vs. the new {method: flags}[] format) and includes a comment in bootstrap-lazy explaining the compatibility rationale.

* fix lint errors

* Update src/runtime/normalize-watchers.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): rename set-value.spec.ts to normalize-watchers.spec.ts

commit.message:
feat: implement complete LIOS legal intelligence operating system

Add full Python implementation of LIOS (Legal Intelligence Operating System)
for EU sustainability regulation compliance (CSRD, ESRS, EU Taxonomy, SFDR).

Features implemented:
- Three-agent consensus engine (sustainability, supply chain, finance agents)
  running in parallel with configurable agreement threshold (≥2 of 3)
- Regulatory Decay Scoring (freshness score 0-100 based on days since update)
- Jurisdiction Conflict Detection (EU vs national law: CSRD/HGB, Taxonomy/PACTE,
  SFDR/WpHG, and more)
- Citation Engine with relevance scoring and EUR-Lex URLs
- Compliance Roadmap Generator with phased deadlines per company profile
- Applicability Checker for CSRD, ESRS, EU Taxonomy, SFDR
- Conflict Mapper matrix across jurisdiction/regulation pairs
- Legal Breakdown Generator with obligations, penalties, and timelines

Architecture:
- lios/knowledge/: Structured regulatory data (CSRD, ESRS, EU Taxonomy, SFDR)
  with 40+ real articles based on actual EU law
- lios/agents/: BaseAgent with rule-based fallback (no LLM required),
  three specialist agents, ConsensusEngine
- lios/features/: Seven analytical feature modules
- lios/orchestration/: QueryParser (intent detection), ResponseAggregator,
  OrchestrationEngine routing all components
- lios/api/routes.py: FastAPI REST API (6 endpoints)
- lios/cli/interface.py: Click CLI (5 commands including 'lios serve')
- tests/: 121 pytest tests covering all modules
